steps.title: Identify the structural requirement for the relative clause
steps.content: After “whose body of [blank],” the word “interdisciplinary” (an adjective) appears with no verb between it and its subject. The relative clause introduced by “whose” needs a finite verb to form a complete predicate with its predicate adjective.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb requirement
steps.content:
Only “is” provides a finite verb, creating the correct structure: “whose body of work is interdisciplinary.” Choices A, C, and D provide non-finite forms—an infinitive, a past participle, and a present participle—that cannot serve as the main verb of a relative clause.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
